text: The laparoscopic view shows the inguinal hernia defect with bilateral H, and the iliopubic ligament is visible on the right side of the screen. The surgeon prepares to sew the iliopubic ligament to the abdominal wall muscle. The peritoneum has been opened, revealing the preperitoneal space, and the hernia sac along with the fat inside has been removed, clearly exposing the hernia defect.

The inguinal hernia in a female patient is shown, with the round ligament visible on the left side, passing through the hernia defect. The surgeon successfully repairs the defect using absorbable sutures without mesh. The peritoneum is then closed with absorbable sutures.
